name: Sierra Morena Trail and the Huéznar Waterfalls - circular via San Nicolás del Puerto
name_original: Sendero de Sierra Morena y las Cascadas del Huéznar — circular por San Nicolás del Puerto
region_intro: Jogging routes around San Nicolás Del Puerto traverse the diverse landscapes of the Sierra Norte de Sevilla Natural Park. This region features extensive meadows, forests of holm oaks and cork oaks, and riparian forests along rivers like the Huéznar and Galindón. The terrain includes unique karst formations at Cerro del Hierro and offers varied paths suitable for running. Best jogging routes around San Nicolás Del Puerto The most popular jogging route is The Los Sendero de Sierra Morena y del Molino del Corcho - from Cazalla de la Sierra — Parque Natural Sierra Norte de Sevilla, a 7.3 miles (11.8 km) trail that takes 1 hour 28 minutes to complete. This route offers a journey through the varied forest and meadow landscapes of the Sierra Norte de Sevilla Natural Park. Another top favourite among local runners is Sierra Morena Trail and the Huéznar Waterfalls - circular via San Nicolás del Puerto, a difficult 9.7 miles (15.6 km) path. This circular route includes the scenic Huéznar Waterfalls and follows the Rivera del Huéznar. Local runners also love the Cerro del Hierro Circular Trail, a 3.9 miles (6.3 km) trail leading through the unique karst landscape of Cerro del Hierro, often completed in about 42 minutes. Jogging around San Nicolás Del Puerto is defined by extensive dehesas, dense forests, and river valleys, including the unique rock formations of Cerro del Hierro. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy paths to more challenging trails. The routes in San Nicolás Del Puerto are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 4 reviews. More than 90 runners have used komoot to explore San Nicolás Del Puerto's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around San Nicolás Del Puerto?
A: There are over 20 dedicated jogging routes around San Nicolás Del Puerto, offering a variety of experiences through the Sierra Norte de Sevilla Natural Park. These routes cater to different fitness levels, from easy paths to more challenging trails.

Q: What is the overall difficulty level of running trails in San Nicolás Del Puerto?
A: The running trails in San Nicolás Del Puerto offer a good mix of difficulties. You'll find 1 easy route, 15 moderate routes, and 4 difficult routes, ensuring options for both casual joggers and experienced runners seeking a challenge.

Q: Are there any easy running routes suitable for beginners in San Nicolás Del Puerto?
A: Yes, there is at least one easy running route available. The area's diverse terrain also includes flatter sections, particularly along river valleys and the Sierra Norte Greenway, which can be suitable for beginners or those looking for a less strenuous run.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in San Nicolás Del Puerto?
A: The running routes in San Nicolás Del Puerto are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 4 reviews. More than 90 runners have already explored the varied terrain, often praising the natural beauty and tranquility of the Sierra Norte de Sevilla Natural Park.

Q: Can I find running routes that pass by the scenic Huéznar Waterfalls?
A: Absolutely! The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1013642270/sierra-morena-trail-and-the-hueznar-waterfalls-circular-via-san-nicolas-del-puerto" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra Morena Trail and the Huéznar Waterfalls - circular via San Nicolás del Puerto&lt;/a&gt; is a popular option. This challenging 9.7-mile (15.6 km) circular route includes the stunning &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4764750" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Huéznar Waterfalls&lt;/a&gt;, a Natural Monument, and follows the picturesque Rivera del Huéznar.

Q: Are there running trails near the unique rock formations of Cerro del Hierro?
A: Yes, you can explore the fascinating karst landscape of Cerro del Hierro while running. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1013643037/cerro-del-hierro-circular-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro del Hierro Circular Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a 3.9-mile (6.3 km) route that takes you through this unique area, often described as a 'destroyed city' due to its intriguing rock formations. You can also visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4781135" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro del Hierro Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; for panoramic views.

Q: Are there any trails that follow a greenway or old railway line in the area?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4781503" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra Norte de Sevilla Greenway&lt;/a&gt;, a former railway line, offers a flat and accessible route perfect for jogging. It spans over 19 kilometers and provides scenic views, passing through parts of the Cerro del Hierro monument and historical mining buildings.

Q: What natural landmarks can I see while jogging in the San Nicolás Del Puerto region?
A: Beyond the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4764750" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Huéznar Waterfalls&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4781135" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro del Hierro Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, you can encounter the extensive dehesas and riparian forests of the Sierra Norte de Sevilla Natural Park. The area also features the historic Roman Bridge over the River Galindón and the unique &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4764725" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Nicolás del Puerto River Beach&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in San Nicolás Del Puerto?
A: Yes, several routes are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1013643037/cerro-del-hierro-circular-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro del Hierro Circular Trail&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1013642270/sierra-morena-trail-and-the-hueznar-waterfalls-circular-via-san-nicolas-del-puerto" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra Morena Trail and the Huéznar Waterfalls - circular via San Nicolás del Puerto&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I find running routes that are suitable for families with children?
A: While specific 'family-friendly' routes are not explicitly listed, the Sierra Norte Greenway offers a flat and accessible path that could be suitable for families. Additionally, some of the easier, shorter routes or sections of trails near the town or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4764688" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Huéznar Spring Recreation Area&lt;/a&gt; might be enjoyable for a family jog.

Q: Are there any long-distance running routes for experienced runners?
A: For experienced runners looking for a longer challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20122024" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Molino del Corcho Area loop from Cazalla de la Sierra&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 15.1-mile (24.3 km) route with significant elevation gain. This trail offers an extensive exploration of the natural park's varied landscapes.

Q: What kind of landscapes will I encounter on the running trails?
A: Jogging routes around San Nicolás Del Puerto traverse the diverse landscapes of the Sierra Norte de Sevilla Natural Park. You'll encounter extensive meadows (dehesas) interspersed with forests of holm oaks, cork oaks, gall oaks, chestnuts, pines, and elms, as well as lush riparian forests along rivers like the Huéznar and Galindón. The unique karst formations at Cerro del Hierro also add to the varied scenery.
tips: In this small town with cobbled streets is the Iglesia de San Sebastian Y San Diego which is located in the old center of the town!

Las Cascadas del Huéznar is an area of the river of the same name where there are a series of waterfalls and pools that arise when the water falls on peculiar limestone formations known as travertines. Along these waterfalls, also called Las Chorreras, the river runs along its course with abundant crystal clear water. This set of waterfalls was declared a Natural Monument in 2001.

This stone bridge dates back to the time of the Roman conquest, although it has subsequently undergone some renovations. It is located on the Galindón River and currently only pedestrian and bicycle traffic is allowed.
